title: Modularity lifting theorems for non-regular symplectic representations
speaker: George Boxer
abstract: Abstract: We prove an ordinary modularity lifting theorem for certain non-regular 4-dimensional symplectic representations over totally real fields. The argument uses both higher Hida theory and the Calegari-Geraghty version of the Taylor-Wiles method. We also present some applications of these theorems to abelian surfaces. (Joint work with F. Calegari, T. Gee, and V. Pilloni.)

proof_stages.label: abelian-surface modularity problem and Galois representations
proof_stages.summary: The talk is framed by the conjectural correspondence between cuspidal GSp4 automorphic representations and abelian surfaces over totally real fields, emphasizing that although actual abelian surfaces cannot generally be constructed from automorphic forms, both sides have compatible degree-four Galois representations.

proof_stages.label: non-regular GSp4 modularity lifting theorem
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is stated for a p-adic GSp4 Galois representation over a totally real field with cyclotomic multiplier, ordinary non-regular Hodge-Tate weights 0,0,1,1, residual weight-two modularity, big residual image, and purity, concluding that the representation is modular.

proof_stages.label: potential modularity consequences
proof_stages.summary: The modularity lifting theorem is used to prove potential modularity of abelian surfaces over totally real fields, giving meromorphic continuation and the expected functional equation by Brauer induction and implying potential modularity for elliptic curves over quadratic extensions of totally real fields.

proof_stages.label: weight-one GL2 model for non-regularity
proof_stages.summary: The Buzzard-Taylor theorem for odd Artin representations is recalled as the GL2 analogue, where coherent H1 obstructs weight-one congruences, ordinary p-adic modular forms remove the obstruction, and a two-eigenvalue gluing argument recovers classicality.

proof_stages.label: derived patching strategy and its GSp4 obstruction
proof_stages.summary: The Calegari-Geraghty idea of patching a cohomology complex rather than only H0 is transplanted to the Hilbert-Siegel variety, but the required Galois representations, local-global compatibility, and cohomological amplitude bounds are unavailable in the needed generality.

proof_stages.label: hybrid two-degree patching and p-adic gluing
proof_stages.summary: The proof instead patches only mixed ordinary/Klingen complexes indexed by subsets I of p-adic places in the case |I|=1, where cohomology has two relevant degrees, and then uses the resulting partial R=T statements as the input for a p-adic gluing argument.

examples.name: computational modular abelian surfaces over Q
examples.note: The known finite list of general-type abelian surfaces over Q, including a conductor-277 case, illustrates how little is known unconditionally and that existing modularity proofs proceed by first finding the automorphic form computationally.

examples.name: elliptic curves over Q(2^(1/4))
examples.note: The mixed-signature quadratic extension k/F with k=Q(2^(1/4)) and F=Q(sqrt(2)) illustrates the new range of the potential modularity corollary for elliptic curves over non-totally-real, non-CM fields.
